other_observers: J. Geisler, S. Spence, and a couple others, none of which you would know, anyway.; Yes, I am the original observer. This was a serendipitous observation, as I was at the lake photographing Clark's and western grebes.
description: Size: about 7-8 in long  Shape: phalarope-like, based on previous experience with Wilson's phalarope.  Bill: short, fairly thick (not like photos of red-necked I have seen); mainly dark but yellowish at base.  Plumage: rufous/reddish on flanks and neck; brownish back; blackish on head. But none of the colors were uniform, as the bird appeared to be transitioning to winter plumage.  I have a picture of the bird, which I will try to upload.Behaviors: Observed only for a very short time before it flew away. It was resting on the water at Empire reservoir, near Wiggins, CO.Call: not heardPlumage: transitioning from breeding to winter
